Diana Reed Slattery is a practicing writer, VJ, and xenolinguist. She is currently working on her PhD with the Planetary Collegium on the topic of “Communicating the Unspeakable: Linguistic Phenomena in the Psychedelic Sphere.” She is the author of a novel, The Maze Game . Graham St John is a cultural anthropologist who researches electronic dance music cultures, festivals, and movements. His forthcoming book Global Tribe: Spirituality, Technology and Psytrance is out with Equinox in 2012. His other books include Technomad: Global Raving Countercultures (Equinox 2009), The Local Scenes and Global Culture of Psytrance (Routledge 2010), and FreeNRG: Notes From the Edge of the Dance Floor (Common Ground 2001). He is the executive editor of Dancecult: Journal of Electronic Dance Music Culture ( www.dj.dancecult.net ). His website is at www.edgecentral.net and his blog at www.edgecentral.blogspot.com . Daenin Tajeda currently lives in Seattle. He teaches people how to become energy aware and how to wake up their unique gifts. He is a healer and teacher with the Modern Mystery School in the Lineage of King Solomon. He is into high-vibration nutrition and is currently studying at the Institute for Integrative Nutrition to become a health counselor. He looks for cocreators and collaborators to form community-based action teams of conscious humanitarians. His agenda: share gifts and raise consciousness so we can create new solutions to old problems. Russell Targ , born in Chicago and raised in New York City, is a physicist and author who was a pioneer in the development of the laser and laser applications, and was co-founder of the Stanford Research Institute’s investigation into psychic abilities in the 1970s and 1980s. His work in this new area, called remote viewing, was published in Nature, The Proceedings of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), and the Proceedings of the American Association for the Advancement of Science . Targ has a BS in Math and Physics from Queens College and did graduate work in physics at Columbia University. He is the coauthor of seven books dealing with the scientific investigation of psychic abilities. Targ has a brand-new book called The Reality of ESP: A Physicist’s Proof of Psychic Abilities , and his website is www.espresearch.com . Michael Taussig is an anthropologist known for his provocative ethnographic studies and unconventional style as an academic. He was born in 1940 in Australia, where he studied medicine at the University of Sydney. He is currently a professor of anthropology at Columbia University in New York. In spite of his numerous publications in his field, especially in medical anthropology, he is most acclaimed for his commentaries on Karl Marx and Walter Benjamin, especially in relation to the idea of commodity fetishism. He is the author of the following books: What Color is the Sacred? (2009), Walter Benjamin’s Grave (2006), My Cocaine Museum (2004), Law in a Lawless Land: Diary of a Limpieza in a Colombian Town (2003), Defacement (1999), Magic of the State (1997), Mimesis and Alterity: A Particular History of the Senses (1993), The Nervous System (1992), Shamanism, Colonialism, and the Wild Man: A Study in Terror and Healing (1987), and The Devil and Commodity Fetishism in South America (1980). Tony Vigorito holds a doctorate in social theory and is the author of the novels Just a Couple of Days and Nine Kinds of Naked . He is currently finishing his third novel. Check out www.tonyvigorito.com . Alberto Villoldo , the author of numerous best-selling books, is a psychologist and medical anthropologist who has studied the spiritual practices of the Amazon and the Andes for more than twenty-five years. While at San Francisco State University, he founded the Biological Self-Regulation Laboratory to study how the mind creates psychosomatic health and disease. Dr. Villoldo directs The Four Winds Society, where he instructs individuals throughout the world in the practice of energy medicine and soul retrieval. He has training centers in New England; California; the UK; the Netherlands; and Park City, Utah. An avid skier, hiker, and mountaineer, he leads annual expeditions to the Amazon and the Andes to work with the wisdom teachers of the Americas. Robert Waggoner is author of the award-winning Lucid Dreaming: Gateway to the Inner Self . An experienced lucid dreamer with approximately one thousand lucid dreams, he coedits the online quarterly e-zine The Lucid Dream Exchange . Waggoner is a past president of the International Association for the Study of Dreams. Timothy Wyllie was born in London in 1940, educated in England, and qualified as an architect before moving to America in 1967. In 1973 he had a full-blown Near Death Experience in which he saw and heard angels, experienced his two companion angels, and was healed and returned to life. He is the author of numerous books, including Dolphins, ETs & Angels , which was published first in 1984 and is still in print. His book Adventures Among Spiritual Intelligences continues the author’s study of angels, extraterrestrials, dolphins, and shamanism; the third book in this series, The Return of the Rebel Angels , was published in October 2011. The Helianx Proposition, Or, The Return of the Rainbow Serpent , published by Daynal Institute Press in 2012, is an illustrated cosmic creation myth that explores the deeper symbolic resonance of the Serpent in the Garden of Eden legend. The first volume of his latest book, The Confessions of a Rebel Angel , will be published by Inner Traditions/Bear & Co. in 2013. Wyllie has lived in the high desert of New Mexico since 1990. ABOUT THE EDITORS Daniel Pinchbeck is the editorial director of Reality Sandwich and cofounder of Evolver.net . A founder of the literary magazine Open City , he has also written for Esquire, The New York Times Magazine, The Village Voice , and Rolling Stone .
